host_state_ownership.current_world_time_location: <Does your game own current time/location? If yes, name the state path/tool. If no, RPG memory may record it as a fact/history.>
host_state_ownership.player_progression: <Level/XP/attributes owner: host tool/state or RPG memory?>

event_types.state_patch: <true only if RPG memory is allowed to mirror state changes>
event_types.mechanical_state: <true only if host has no authoritative mechanics engine>
event_types.inventory_state: <true only if host has no inventory truth source>
event_types.equipment_state: <true only if host has no equipment truth source>
event_types.skill_state: <true only if host has no skill truth source>
event_types.economy_state: <true only if host has no economy truth source>
event_types.progression_state: <true only if host has no progression truth source>
event_types.combat_state: <true only if host has no combat runtime truth source>

sections.current_state: <true only if RPG memory owns or mirrors current state>

conflict_policy.rule: RPG memory may store any enabled domain, but it must not override a host-owned truth source.
host_owned_state_paths: <fill with paths owned by your game, e.g. /player/hp>
host_authoritative_tools: <fill with tools whose results override memory, e.g. get_status>
do_not_store_current_values_for: <fields that memory may mention narratively but must not store as current state>
allowed_narrative_examples: A promise was made before witnesses.
An item became important as a keepsake, without claiming current inventory count.
A battle outcome is remembered, without storing current HP.
